description: This figure presents EBSD characterization and grain size analysis of aged Fe-0.25C-3Mn-2Al low-density steel samples: AN750-T (750℃ intercritical annealing + 400℃ aging), AN800-T (800℃ intercritical annealing + 400℃ aging), AN850-T (850℃ intercritical annealing + 400℃ aging). Subfigures (a) and (b) show inverse pole figure (IPF) orientation color maps (20 μm scale bar, color coded for <001>, <101>, <111> crystal orientations) before parent grain reconstruction and after parent grain analysis, respectively. Subfigure (c) shows equivalent circle diameter distributions of deformation-induced martensite (DIM) for each sample, comparing pre-reconstruction (red curve) and post parent grain analysis (black curve) results. Measured average DIM sizes are 3.29/3.41 μm (Δ=0.12) for AN750-T, 2.94/3.09 μm (Δ=0.15) for AN800-T, 2.61/2.80 μm (Δ=0.19) for AN850-T pre/post reconstruction. The data demonstrates average DIM size decreases with increasing annealing temperature, while deviation between pre and post reconstruction values rises, supporting quantitative microstructure analysis of TRIP effect in the multiphase low-density steel.
